title: Simplify GPU sketch batch sizing
body:
This changes GPU sketch batching to use a fixed sliding-window size instead of the previous memory-estimation path.

Changes in this PR:
- remove the memory-estimation-based batch sizing logic
- use a fixed default batch size of `64M` elements, clamped to the input size
- remove the production and test plumbing for overriding the batch size
- remove batching helpers that only supported the old estimator path
- replace estimator-based memory tests with coarse peak-memory regression tests on representative GPU sketch workloads

The goal is to make the batching policy simpler and more stable:
- one fixed batch-size policy
- no dependence on allocator-reported available memory
- less dead code and less testing surface tied to an approximate memory formula

`64M` was chosen from local runtime/peak-memory sweeps as a conservative default that stays in the fast region across the tested shapes without the repeated-merge overhead of smaller batches.

Representative results:

For `65536 x 2048`:
- `16M`: `7.07 GiB`, `469 ms`
- `32M`: `2.38 GiB`, `440 ms`
- `48M`: `1.80 GiB`, `329 ms`
- `64M`: `1.47 GiB`, `287 ms`

For `16384 x 8192`:
- `16M`: `5.21 GiB`, `447 ms`
- `32M`: `3.40 GiB`, `453 ms`
- `48M`: `2.57 GiB`, `383 ms`
- `64M`: `1.98 GiB`, `328 ms`

For `65536 x 512`:
- `16M`: `384 MiB`, `611 ms`
- `32M`: `512 MiB`, `66 ms`
- `64M`: `512 MiB`, `68 ms`
- `80M`: `512 MiB`, `63 ms`

These sweeps show that `16M` is clearly too small, while `64M` consistently avoids the worst repeated-batching overhead and remains near the best runtime/memory region across different column counts.

## Testing
- rebuilt `testxgboost`
- ran focused coverage for GPU sketching, weighted sketching, external-memory sketching, adapter sketching, GPU quantile prune/merge, and all `DeviceSketchWithHessian` cases

Result:
- `61 / 61` passed
author: RAMitchell

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR simplifies GPU quantile sketch batching by removing the prior memory-estimation path and standardizing on a fixed sliding-window batch size (64M elements), along with updating related APIs and tests.

**Changes:**
- Replace memory-estimation-based batch sizing with a fixed `detail::kSketchBatchNumElements` (64M) sliding window.
- Remove batch-size override plumbing from `DeviceSketch*` / `AdapterDeviceSketch` APIs and delete estimator-only helpers.
- Update tests to drop estimator-based assertions and add coarse peak-memory regression ceilings for representative sketch workloads.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `src/common/hist_util.cuh` | Introduces fixed batch constant and removes batch-size override parameters from sketch APIs and adapter sketching. |
| `src/common/hist_util.cu` | Removes estimator implementation and updates `DeviceSketchWithHessian` to use the fixed batch sizing. |
| `tests/cpp/common/test_hist_util.cu` | Replaces estimator/batch-override tests with peak-memory ceiling regression tests; updates call sites for new signatures. |


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (2)</summary>

**src/common/hist_util.cu:279**
* `sketch_batch_num_elements` is derived from `info.num_nonzero_` and can become 0 when `num_nonzero_` is unknown/uninitialized (it is allowed to be 0 in some code paths). If this happens while `page_nnz > 0`, the inner loop `for (...; begin < page_nnz; begin += sketch_batch_num_elements)` will never advance and will hang. Consider clamping the batch size to at least 1, and/or computing the per-page batch size from `page_nnz` (e.g., `batch = min(kSketchBatchNumElements, page_nnz - begin)`), avoiding reliance on `info.num_nonzero_` for loop progress.
```
  std::size_t num_cuts_per_feature = detail::RequiredSampleCutsPerColumn(max_bin, info.num_row_);
  au
Awesome!

commits.message: Use fixed GPU sketch batch size

title: Fix macOS wheel OpenMP dependency resolution
body:
## Summary

This PR fixes the macOS x86_64 wheel CI failure.

The failing job was:

- `Build macOS wheel (macosx_x86_64)`

`delocate` failed while repairing the wheel because `libxgboost.dylib` picked up Homebrew LLVM's `libunwind.1.0.dylib`, and that dependency requires macOS 14.0 even though the wheel target is macOS 10.15.

## Root Cause

On the macOS Intel runner, the wheel build was resolving OpenMP through Homebrew. That introduced a dependency on Homebrew LLVM's `libunwind`, which caused wheel repair to fail with:

```text
delocate.libsana.DelocationError: Library dependencies do not satisfy target MacOS version 10.15:
.../libunwind.1.0.dylib has a minimum target of 14.0
author: RAMitchell

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Fixes macOS x86_64 wheel repair failures by ensuring the build links against a Conda-provided OpenMP runtime (avoiding Homebrew LLVM’s `libunwind` and its higher minimum macOS target). **Changes:** - Install `llvm-openmp` in the macOS wheel build pipeline and prefer the active Conda prefix in `CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH`. - Update the macOS OpenMP CMake logic to prefer `libomp.dylib` from `CONDA_PREFIX` when present, with Homebrew fallback. - Extend the OpenMP RPATH patching to prioritize `$CONDA_PREFIX/lib` during builds in a Conda environment. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | ops/pipeline/build-python-wheels-macos.sh | Installs Conda OpenMP runtime and adjusts prefix search order for wheel builds. | | cmake/FindOpenMPMacOS.cmake | Prefers Conda `libomp` and adjusts RPATH ordering to resolve OpenMP from Conda first. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

title: Prune GPU sketch after each merge
body:
This changes the batched GPU sketching path to keep the intermediate sketch bounded after each local merge.

Changes in this PR:
- always materialize the current batch sketch into a temporary buffer in `Push(...)`
- route all batch integration through `Merge(...)`, including the first window
- add early empty-input fast paths to `Merge(...)`
- prune the resident sketch after each local merge using the intermediate cut budget

The main motivation is memory behavior during batched GPU sketching. Previously, each batch was pruned locally and then merged into the resident sketch, but the merged sketch was not re-pruned until the final cut generation stage. That allowed the resident sketch to grow across many batches.

With this change, the local sketch remains bounded between batches, which makes smaller sketch batches a more effective memory-control knob.

This also simplifies the control flow:
- `Merge(...)` owns the empty-input cases
- `Push(...)` no longer needs a separate first-window specialization

Testing:
- `make -C build-make -j35 testxgboost`
- `./build-make/testxgboost --gtest_filter='GPUQuantile.Basic:GPUQuantile.Prune:GPUQuantile.PruneDuplicated:GPUQuantile.MergeEmpty:GPUQuantile.MergeBasic:GPUQuantile.MergeDuplicated:GPUQuantile.MergeCategorical:GPUQuantile.MultiMerge:Quantile.DistributedBasic:Quantile.Distributed:MGPUQuantileTest.AllReduceBasic'`

Result:
- 10 passed
- 1 skipped (`MGPUQuantileTest.AllReduceBasic`, missing federated/NCCL support)
author: RAMitchell

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR updates the batched GPU quantile sketching workflow to keep the resident sketch size bounded between batches by routing all batch integration through `SketchContainer::Merge(...)` and pruning after each merge. This aims to improve GPU memory behavior when processing many sketch batches. **Changes:** - Always materialize per-batch sketch output into a temporary device buffer inside `SketchContainer::Push(...)`. - Remove the first-window specialization by always merging the batch sketch into the resident sketch. - Prune the resident sketch after each local merge using an intermediate cut budget (`max_bin * kFactor`). --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

title: [python-package] Add specific error codes to all type: ignore comments
body:
## Summary

Replace all 25 generic `# type: ignore` comments across the Python package with specific mypy error codes. This is a follow-up to #6496.

**Changes across 10 files:**

| Error code | Count | Example |
|------------|-------|---------|
| `[union-attr]` | 7 | TypeGuard limitations, optional attribute access, PySpark `set()` |
| `[attr-defined]` | 6 | Deprecated pandas API, numpy dtypes, ctypes CDLL, spark estimator |
| `[no-redef]` | 5 | Conditional class/import redefinitions (3 existing + 2 cudf) |
| `[arg-type]` | 4 | PySpark `mapInPandas()` / `np.stack()`, callback `append()` |
| `[assignment]` | 2 | Type narrowing, `None` assignment |
| `[return-value]` | 1 | `_load_lib()` returning `None` for doc builds |
| `[name-defined]` | 1 | `pd.arrays.ArrowExtensionArray` not in stubs |
| `[call-overload]` | 1 | PySpark `@pandas_udf` decorator |
| `[index, typeddict-unknown-key]` | 1 | CUDA stream on `Optional[ArrayInf]` |

**Why:** Generic `type: ignore` silently masks *any* type error on that line, including new unrelated errors introduced by future changes. Specific error codes ensure only the known issue is suppressed.

No functional changes — only comment annotations.

## Test plan

- [x] CI pre-commit passes
- [x] CI mypy type check passes — all error codes verified against actual mypy output

title: [python-package] Handle indicator (boolean) features in trees_to_dataframe
body:
## Summary

`trees_to_dataframe()` crashes with `ValueError: Failed to parse model text dump`
when the feature map contains indicator type (`'i'`) features.

The C++ text dump produces three formats for split nodes:

| Type | Format | Python parser |
|------|--------|---------------|
| Quantitative | `0:[f<0.5] yes=1,no=2,missing=1,gain=...,cover=...` | Handled |
| Categorical | `0:[f:{0,1}] yes=1,no=2,missing=1,gain=...,cover=...` | Handled |
| **Indicator** | `0:[f] yes=1,no=2,gain=...,cover=...` | **Not handled** |

The indicator format has no `<` or `:{`, no split threshold, and no `missing=`
field. The parser now recognizes this third format and correctly sets `Split`
and `Missing` to NaN for indicator nodes.

## Test plan

- Added `test_tree_to_df_indicator` in `test_parse_tree.py`
- Trains on binary features with an indicator feature map
- Verifies the DataFrame is produced without error
- Verifies indicator nodes have NaN splits and NaN missing

Closes https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/10437
author: PavelGuzenfeld

reviews.body: Copilot encountered an error and was unable to review this pull request. You can try again by re-requesting a review.

## Pull request overview Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
Handle indicator (boolean) features in trees_to_dataframe

trees_to_dataframe crashes with ValueError when the feature map
contains indicator type ('i') features, because the text dump format
for indicator nodes differs from quantitative and categorical:

  Quantitative: 0:[fname<0.5] yes=1,no=2,missing=1,gain=...,cover=...
  Categorical:  0:[fname:{0,1}] yes=1,no=2,missing=1,gain=...,cover=...
  Indicator:    0:[fname] yes=1,no=2,gain=...,cover=...

The indicator format has no split threshold, no curly braces, and no
missing direction. The parser now recognizes this format and sets
split and missing to NaN for indicator nodes.

Fix ruff lint and bitwise XOR on float32 in indicator test

Remove blank line between import groups (ruff check) and cast
float32 slices to int32 before applying bitwise XOR to fix
TypeError on numpy versions that enforce safe casting.
Fix ruff format: join single-line expression

Address review feedback: fix XOR test, add indicator validation, use explicit encoding

- Compute XOR on integer array before float32 cast to avoid TypeError
- Add validation in indicator else-branch so unrecognized formats still raise ValueError
- Use explicit encoding="utf-8" when writing the feature map file

[python-package] Set indicator missing direction to no-child, assert non_leaf non-empty

For indicator splits, the C++ text dump encodes the default (missing)
child as the "no" direction. Set Missing to match No instead of NaN.
Also assert non_leaf is non-empty so the test guarantees coverage of
indicator split parsing.

title: [python-package] Fix python -OO crash by guarding __doc__ assignments
body:
## Summary

Fixes an import crash when running `python -OO` (or `PYTHONOPTIMIZE=2`) by guarding all runtime `__doc__` assignments against `None`.

Under `-OO`, Python strips all docstrings so `__doc__` is `None`. The existing code:
- Uses f-strings interpolating `Booster.save_model.__doc__` / `Booster.load_model.__doc__` (produces `None` in output)  
- Asserts `XGBModel.fit.__doc__ is not None` then calls `.replace()` on it (crashes with `AssertionError`)
- Copies `__doc__` from sklearn wrappers in dask (assigns `None` — harmless but pointless)

This PR wraps each `__doc__` assignment with `if source.__doc__ is not None:` so:
- Under `-OO`: assignments are silently skipped, no crash
- Under normal Python: docstrings work exactly as before

Closes #8537

Supersedes #12085 (reworked based on review feedback — previous approach removed docstrings entirely, this one preserves them)

## Test plan
- [x] `python -OO -c "import xgboost"` no longer crashes
- [x] Normal `python -c "import xgboost; help(xgboost.XGBClassifier.fit)"` still shows full docstrings
author: PavelGuzenfeld

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Fixes an import-time crash when running XGBoost under `python -OO` / `PYTHONOPTIMIZE=2` by ensuring runtime `__doc__` reassignments are skipped when the source docstring is `None` (as happens when docstrings are stripped). **Changes:** - Guard `save_model` / `load_model` docstring copying from `Booster` against `None`. - Guard `XGBClassifier.fit.__doc__` rewriting (previously relied on an `assert` and `.replace()`). - Guard docstring copying for Dask wrappers (`predict_proba`, `fit`) against `None`. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | python-package/xgboost/sklearn.py | Adds `None` checks around `Booster.*.__doc__` copies and `XGBModel.fit.__doc__`-based rewrite to prevent `-OO` import crashes. | | python-package/xgboost/dask/__init__.py | Adds `None` checks for docstring copying from sklearn wrappers to avoid `-OO` issues and no-op assignments. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
Fix python -OO crash by guarding __doc__ assignments against None

Under `python -OO`, docstrings are stripped and `__doc__` is None.
Code that reads `__doc__` from one object to copy or mutate it for
another would crash with an AttributeError (e.g. calling `.replace()`
on None).

Wrap each `__doc__` assignment with a `if source.__doc__ is not None`
guard so the assignment is simply skipped under -OO, while preserving
full docstring behavior under normal Python.

title: Replace GPU quantile double buffering with explicit scratch storage
body:
This continues the GPU sketching refactor by making the sketch container state explicit.

Changes in this PR:
- replace the implicit alternating double-buffer model with one primary sketch buffer and one explicit scratch buffer
- do the same for the column-pointer storage
- keep the prune/merge behavior unchanged from the previous PR in the stack
- keep the change focused on container state management and readability

The intent is to make the GPU sketch container easier to reason about:
- one buffer is the current sketch state
- one buffer is scratch/output storage for out-of-place transforms
- commit points are explicit instead of relying on hidden buffer alternation

This is part of the larger GPU sketching refactor. The broader direction is to separate:
- prune behavior
- merge behavior
- container/buffer state management

Impact:
I compared this branch against `upstream/master` after `#12099` landed, using a temporary adapter-based GPU sketch benchmark on a `20,000 x 512` input with `256` bins.

Results:
- `upstream/master`
  - median sketch time: `11.313 ms`
  - sketch container capacity: `52,453,392` bytes
  - sketch container live cost: `52,315,424` bytes
- this branch
  - median sketch time: `11.347 ms`
  - sketch container capacity: `52,453,392` bytes
  - sketch container live cost: `52,315,424` bytes

So this change did not show a meaningful runtime or memory impact in the measured sketch path. That is expected: the PR changes how the state is represented, not how many sketch buffers exist.

Testing:
- `make -C build-make -j35 testxgboost`
- `./build-make/testxgboost --gtest_filter='GPUQuantile.Basic:GPUQuantile.Prune:GPUQuantile.PruneDuplicated:GPUQuantile.MergeEmpty:GPUQuantile.MergeBasic:GPUQuantile.MergeDuplicated:GPUQuantile.MergeCategorical:GPUQuantile.MultiMerge:Quantile.DistributedBasic:Quantile.Distributed:MGPUQuantileTest.AllReduceBasic'`

Result:
- 10 passed
- 1 skipped (`MGPUQuantileTest.AllReduceBasic`, missing federated/NCCL support)
author: RAMitchell

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Refactors the GPU quantile `SketchContainer` to make buffer ownership and “commit” points explicit, replacing the implicit alternating double-buffer pattern with a primary sketch buffer plus explicit scratch storage. This is part of the ongoing GPU sketching refactor to improve readability and reasoning about container state without changing prune/merge semantics. **Changes:** - Replace implicit alternating entry buffers with `entries_` (current) + `entries_tmp_` (scratch) and add explicit commit behavior. - Replace the secondary column-pointer storage with `columns_ptr_tmp_` and route scan/merge/prune output through the scratch pointers. - Update merge/prune to use the explicit scratch/commit flow and remove buffer alternation. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `src/common/quantile.cuh` | Reworks `SketchContainer` storage to explicit current/scratch buffers and adds commit helpers/accessors. | | `src/common/quantile.cu` | Updates scan/prune/merge implementations to write into scratch buffers and explicitly commit results. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: Inline categorical compaction after GPU sketch merge
body:
This continues the GPU sketching refactor by narrowing the role of `Unique()` and moving merge cleanup closer to where it is needed.

Changes in this PR:
- remove `SketchContainer::Unique()`
- keep numeric value dedup local to `Prune(...)`
- inline categorical-only compaction directly into `Merge(...)`
- make `Merge(...)` own `FixError()`
- remove redundant caller-side `FixError()` / `Unique()` calls in tests and distributed merge paths

The intent is to make the GPU sketch pipeline responsibilities clearer:
- `Prune(...)` handles summary-side numeric cleanup
- `Merge(...)` handles post-merge categorical cleanup and rank-bound repair
- callers no longer need to remember extra normalization steps after merge

This is part of the larger GPU sketching refactor. The broader direction is to separate:
- prune/selection behavior
- merge behavior
- container/buffer cleanup

Keeping this PR focused on `Unique()` narrowing and merge ownership should make the later container changes easier to review.

## Testing
- `make -C build-make -j35 testxgboost`
- `./build-make/testxgboost --gtest_filter='GPUQuantile.Basic:GPUQuantile.Prune:GPUQuantile.PruneDuplicated:GPUQuantile.MergeEmpty:GPUQuantile.MergeBasic:GPUQuantile.MergeDuplicated:GPUQuantile.MultiMerge:Quantile.DistributedBasic:Quantile.Distributed:MGPUQuantileTest.AllReduceBasic'`

Result:
- 9 passed
- 1 skipped (`MGPUQuantileTest.AllReduceBasic`, missing federated/NCCL support)
author: RAMitchell

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR refactors the GPU quantile sketch normalization pipeline by removing `SketchContainer::Unique()` and relocating deduplication/normalization responsibilities into `Prune(...)` (numeric-side cleanup) and `Merge(...)` (categorical compaction + rank invariant repair via `FixError()`), with corresponding test updates to stop requiring caller-side normalization steps. **Changes:** - Removed `SketchContainer::Unique()` from the sketch container API and inlined its behavior into `Prune(...)` (general dedup after selection) and `Merge(...)` (categorical-only compaction after merge). - Made `Merge(...)` responsible for calling `FixError()` and removed redundant caller/test invocations. - Updated GPU quantile tests to validate ordering/invariants without explicit `Unique()` / `FixError()` calls. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `tests/cpp/common/test_quantile.cu` | Removes `Unique()`-specific tests/calls and adjusts merge tests to validate invariants directly post-`Merge()`. | | `src/common/quantile.cuh` | Deletes the `SketchContainer::Unique()` method from the container interface. | | `src/common/quantile.cu` | Inlines segmented-unique logic into `Prune(...)`, adds categorical-only compaction into `Merge(...)`, and moves `FixError()` ownership into `Merge(...)`. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: Make GPU sketch prune emit a correct summary
body:
This is a focused cleanup in the GPU sketch `Prune(...)` path.

The main change is to separate two roles that were previously mixed together:
- pruning raw pushed entries
- pruning existing sketch summaries

For the summary-side `Prune(...)` path, this PR now:
- selects source indices for the pruned summary
- removes repeated selected indices within each column
- gathers the selected `SketchEntry` values into the output summary

This is closer to the CPU `SetPrune` structure, where prune selection is based on selected records instead of immediately materializing output and then relying on that materialization for all cleanup.

This PR does **not** remove the later value-based `Unique()` step. It turns out that is still needed for now, especially in distributed / merged-summary cases. The value of this PR is separating out the summary prune implementation so later cleanup steps can be changed more safely and incrementally.

This is also one part of a larger refactoring of the GPU sketching path. The broader direction is to separate:
- summary selection under the prune budget
- summary normalization / cleanup
- container and merge-path cleanup

**Testing**
- `make -C build-make -j35 testxgboost`
- `./build-make/testxgboost --gtest_filter='GPUQuantile.Prune:GPUQuantile.PruneDuplicated:GPUQuantile.MergeBasic:GPUQuantile.MultiMerge:Quantile.DistributedBasic:Quantile.Distributed'`

All 6 tests passed.
author: RAMitchell

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR updates the GPU quantile sketch pruning path to avoid producing duplicated cut entries (especially under highly repeated input values) and extends the GPU quantile tests to validate the stronger post-prune invariants. **Changes:** - Refactors pruning in `SketchContainer::Prune` to select/gather pruned entries via index selection and a segmented-unique pass, instead of pruning then calling `Unique()`. - Renames the device-side binary search helper to return an index (`BinarySearchQueryIndex`) and adapts pruning to use it. - Expands GPU quantile tests to assert per-column no-duplicate cut values after pruning, including a new repeated-values test case. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `src/common/quantile.cu` | Refactors GPU sketch pruning to de-duplicate by selected source indices and shrinks output accordingly. | | `tests/cpp/common/test_quantile.cu` | Strengthens prune validation (per-column duplicate checks) and adds a test targeting repeated-value inputs. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

title: Serialize the RNGs
body:
This PR serializes the random states used by the context and by the column sampler. The column sampler stores a state, as we need to broadcast the seed for distributed training. Please note that the configuration is not stable across releases. The `Learner` class discards the configuration automatically if the versions don't match.

*todos*
- [x] Check accuracy is not changed.
- [x] Check cross platform compatibility.
- [x] Unify the code paths for rng serialization.

Related:
- https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/12037
- https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/10324
- https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/12043

Close:
- https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/6711
- https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10354
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR extends XGBoost’s checkpoint/training-continuation determinism by persisting RNG state in the runtime `Context` and by persisting the `ColumnSampler` RNG/seed via updater configs, with added Python property-based tests to guard against regressions.

**Changes:**
- Serialize/deserialize `Context` RNG state into config JSON (`rng_state`) and wire it into learner config save/load.
- Persist `ColumnSampler` seed + RNG state in hist/approx updaters’ `SaveConfig`/`LoadConfig`, and avoid re-broadcasting a new seed when restoring from config.
- Add Hypothesis-based CPU/GPU tests that compare single-session vs continued-session training outputs and RNG state.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 13 out of 13 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `src/context.cc` | Adds `Context::ToJson/FromJson` that include `rng_state` serialization. |
| `include/xgboost/context.h` | Exposes `Context::ToJson/FromJson` declarations (forward-declares `Json`). |
| `src/learner.cc` | Uses `ctx_.ToJson/FromJson` in config save/load; seeds per-iteration RNG using `BoostedRounds()`. |
| `src/common/random.h` / `src/common/random.cc` | Adds `ColumnSampler` seed tracking + JSON save/load of seed and RNG state. |
| `src/tree/updater_quantile_hist.cc` | Saves/loads `column_sampler` state and reuses it on update when present. |
| `src/tree/updater_approx.cc` | Same `column_sampler` config persistence/reuse for approx updater. |
| `src/tree/updater_gpu_hist.cu` | Same `column_sampler` config persistence/reuse for GPU hist/approx updaters. |
| `src/tree/hist/sampler.h` / `src/tree/hist/sampler.cc` | Removes unused includes related to prior RNG usage. |
| `python-package/xgboost/testing/continuation.py` | Adds `run_training_continuation_determinism` helper. |
| `tests/python/test_training_continuation.py` | Adds Hypothesis determinism

## Pull request overview

This PR extends XGBoost’s checkpoint/config serialization to persist RNG state from both `Context` and the column sampler, aiming to improve determinism for training continuation (including distributed column sampling).

**Changes:**
- Add JSON serialization/deserialization for `Context` RNG state and for `common::ColumnSampler` (seed + RNG state).
- Teach multiple tree updaters (CPU/GPU hist/approx/exact) to save/load an optional `column_sampler` config block.
- Expand training-continuation determinism tests (Hypothesis) and extend cross-platform CI to include an additional artifact.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 17 out of 17 changed files in this pull request and generated 7 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `src/common/random.cc` | Adds `SaveRng/LoadRng` plus `ColumnSampler::{SaveConfig,LoadConfig}` and optional loader helper. |
| `src/common/random.h` | Adds column sampler seed storage + config APIs; declares RNG JSON helpers. |
| `src/context.cc` | Adds `Context::{ToJson,FromJson}` that includes RNG state. |
| `include/xgboost/context.h` | Declares `Context::{ToJson,FromJson}` (and forward-declares `Json`). |
| `src/learner.cc` | Switches generic param serialization to `ctx_.ToJson()/FromJson()`; adjusts seed-per-iteration behavior to use `BoostedRounds()`. |
| `src/tree/updater_quantile_hist.cc` | Saves/loads optional `column_sampler` in updater config; constructor signature adjusted for MT builder. |
| `src/tree/updater_gpu_hist.cu` | Saves/loads optional `column_sampler`; avoids recreating it if already loaded. |
| `src/tree/updater_approx.cc` | Saves/loads optional `column_sampler`; creates it lazily if missing. |
| `src/tree/updater_colmaker.cc` | Persists a shared `ColumnSampler` at updater scope and passes into per-tree builders; saves/loads it in config. |
| `python-package/xgboost/testing/continuation.py` | Adds Hypothesis strat

The current design leaves us with two separate streams of RNG that we need to track. One inside context and one inside column sampler. Would you consider changing the design of column sampler to require the context rng every time it takes a sample instead of maintaining its own stream? If all random state comes from context rng then we only need to track this to get coherent training after save and load.
LGTM

title: Simplify GPU quantile merge path scan
body:
This simplifies `MergePath` in the GPU quantile sketch merge.

`merge_by_key` now writes the `(x_step, y_step)` tuples consumed by `exclusive_scan_by_key` directly, instead of first materializing a source indicator and then transforming that indicator into scan input.

title: Fix alignment for in-memory allreduce buffer
body:
**Summary**

Fix alignment for the shared buffer used by in-memory collective allreduce.

The previous implementation stored bytes in a `std::string` and then reinterpreted the buffer as typed data for reduction. That provides contiguous storage, but not the alignment guarantees required for typed access. This change switches the shared buffer to aligned storage while preserving the existing byte-oriented behavior for the in-memory collective path.

No intended user-facing changes.

**Verification**

Built `src/collective/in_memory_handler.cc` in the existing `build/` tree.
author: RAMitchell

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Replaces `std::string` with a custom `AlignedByteBuffer` backed by `std::max_align_t` storage to guarantee proper alignment for typed reinterpret_casts during in-memory allreduce. **Changes:** - Introduced `AlignedByteBuffer` class providing aligned byte storage with a `std::string`-like interface - Updated all functor signatures and buffer operations to use `AlignedByteBuffer` - Added alignment/size checks in the allreduce path ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/collective/in_memory_handler.h | Added `AlignedByteBuffer` class; changed `buffer_` member type | | src/collective/in_memory_handler.cc | Updated functors to use `AlignedByteBuffer`; added checks; fixed `Accumulate` signature | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: Move DART state into GBTree
body: This PR is the next step toward removing `GBDart` as a separate booster. It moves the DART runtime state and training-time dropout behavior into `GBTree`, so dropout can be enabled directly through parameters like `rate_drop` without requiring `booster=dart`. The legacy `Dart` class remains as a thin compatibility wrapper for the existing booster name and serialized model/config shape. A follow-up PR will load old DART models/configs directly into unified `GBTree` state and remove the separate `Dart` class.
author: RAMitchell

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR moves DART (Dropout Additive Regression Trees) runtime state and training-time dropout behavior from the separate `Dart` class into the base `GBTree` class. This allows dropout to be enabled directly through parameters like `rate_drop` when using `booster=gbtree`, without requiring `booster=dart`. The `Dart` class is retained as a thin compatibility wrapper for existing serialized models/configs.

**Changes:**
- Moved `weight_drop_`, `idx_drop_`, `dparam_`, `DropTrees()`, `NormalizeTrees()`, and dropout-aware prediction logic from `Dart` into `GBTree`.
- Simplified `Dart` to a thin wrapper that only overrides serialization methods (`SaveModel`, `LoadModel`, `SaveConfig`, `LoadConfig`) and `CommitModel` for backward compatibility with the `"dart"` JSON format.
- Updated documentation (`parameter.rst`, `dart.rst`) and tests to reflect that dropout is now a feature of `gbtree` rather than a separate booster.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| `src/gbm/gbtree.cc` | Moved dropout logic (`DropTrees`, `NormalizeTrees`, weight tracking in `CommitModel`, `PredictBatch`, `InplacePredict`, `Slice`, serialization) into `GBTree`; stripped `Dart` down to a compatibility wrapper. |
| `tests/cpp/gbm/test_gbtree.cc` | Added `GBTree_DropoutJsonIO` test verifying `gbtree` with `rate_drop` saves/loads correctly. |
| `doc/parameter.rst` | Renamed DART section to "Additional dropout parameters for tree boosters" and removed DART-specific predict note. |

title: [coll] Prevent all-to-all connection.
body:
Use a static topology instead. The topology is a union of a ring and a tree.

Close https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10840
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR updates the CPU collective TCP backend to avoid an all-to-all connection pattern by using a fixed, sparse topology (ring neighbors + binomial tree rooted at rank 0), while keeping collective semantics intact (including non-zero-root broadcast via relay). **Changes:** - Introduce sparse peer selection (`SparsePeers`) and sparse channel population (`HasChan` + `Chan` guard) for the TCP communicator. - Rework worker-to-worker connection bootstrap to connect only to the sparse peer set, and adjust tests to account for missing channels. - Update CPU broadcast to use a fixed tree rooted at rank 0 (with relay for non-zero roots) and adjust CPU allgatherV to prefer ring. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | tests/cpp/collective/test_worker.h | Avoids touching non-existent channels in tests by checking `HasChan`. | | src/collective/comm.h | Adds sparse topology helper (`SparsePeers`) and `HasChan`; makes `Chan()` enforce topology. | | src/collective/comm.cc | Changes bootstrap to connect only to sparse peers; stores `nullptr` channels for non-neighbors. | | src/collective/coll.h | Copyright year range update. | | src/collective/broadcast.cc | Implements fixed-tree broadcast rooted at 0 with relay for non-zero roots. | | src/collective/allgather.h | Selects ring allgatherV for CPU and broadcast-based for CUDA. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get star

title: Unify DART SHAP forwarding
body: This PR is another small step toward the follow-up work to remove `GBDart` as a separate booster and treat DART as weighted `GBTree` prediction/training behavior. It removes the DART-specific SHAP forwarding overrides and routes contribution and interaction prediction through the shared `GBTree` path, with DART supplying tree weights through a shared accessor. The scope here is intentionally limited to SHAP forwarding; serialization, training behavior, and booster identity are unchanged. This is groundwork for the larger PR that removes `GBDart`.
author: RAMitchell

reviews.body:

title: Push batch tree weights into predictors
body: This PR is a small step toward the follow-up work to remove GBDart as a separate booster and treat DART as weighted GBTree prediction/training behavior. It moves batch tree weighting into the predictor interface so GBTree::PredictBatchImpl can share one prediction path for both plain GBTree and DART-style weighted prediction. The scope here is intentionally limited to batch prediction; inplace prediction and SHAP are left unchanged for now. This is groundwork for the larger PR that removes GBDart.

## Pull request overview

This PR moves per-tree weighting for **batch prediction** into the `Predictor` interface so `GBTree::PredictBatchImpl` can use a unified prediction path for both plain GBTree and DART-style weighted prediction (as groundwork for removing `GBDart`).

**Changes:**
- Extends `Predictor::PredictBatch` to accept optional per-tree weights and wires weight application into CPU/GPU batch prediction code paths.
- Refactors `GBTree::PredictBatchImpl` and DART’s `PredictBatch` to use a single weighted/unweighted prediction flow, with cache reuse only for unweighted predictions.
- Adds a new unit test exercising weighted batch prediction on both CPU and GPU predictors.

title: Do tree reductions instead of allgather in distributed quantile construction
body:
Implement #12060 

## Summary
This is a WIP draft for replacing distributed sketch merge from allgather-style materialization to a `ReduceV` payload merge path.

Current scope:
- Adds `collective::ReduceV` for variable-size payload reduction.
- Serializes per-feature sketch summaries into a byte payload.
- Merges payloads during reduction and prunes to per-feature cut targets.
- Broadcasts reduced payload back to all workers and reconstructs local reduced summaries.

## Build/Test
Local compile checks on this branch:
- `cmake --build build-cpu-quantile -j20 --target testxgboost` ✅
- `cmake --build build -j20 --target testxgboost` (CUDA, `CMAKE_CUDA_ARCHITECTURES=70`) ✅

## Benchmark (unsorted only)
Compared:
- Current branch implementation (tree-reduce payload merge)
- Baseline allgather implementation (`14ccf336`)

Configuration:
- Test: `Quantile.BenchDistributedLargeSparse`
- `workers=40`, `rows/worker=5000`, `cols=1000`, `bins=256`
- Sparsity: `0.95`, `0.80`
- Repeats: `3`

Metric definitions:
- `max_time_s`: wall-clock time for quantile cut generation (`Push*Page + MakeCuts`) on the slowest worker (`Allreduce(max)`).
- `throughput_nnz_per_s`: total non-zeros across workers divided by `max_time_s`.
- `MAX_RSS_KB`: peak resident set size from `/usr/bin/time` for the full `testxgboost` process.

| Sparsity | Impl | Mean `max_time_s` | Mean `throughput_nnz_per_s` | Mean `MAX_RSS_KB` |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 0.95 | Current | 1.76170 | 5.60802e+06 | 2,437,696 |
| 0.95 | Baseline | 3.07001 | 3.26148e+06 | 6,188,895 |
| 0.80 | Current | 1.67136 | 2.38822e+07 | 3,148,788 |
| 0.80 | Baseline | 7.44744 | 5.31814e+06 | 21,448,572 |

Delta (Current vs Baseline):
- Sparsity `0.95`: `+74.3%` speedup, `+71.9%` throughput, `-60.6%` RSS.
- Sparsity `0.80`: `+345.6%` speedup, `+349.1%` throughput, `-85.3%` RSS.

## Notes
- This PR is intentionally marked `[WIP]` for feedback on API shape and merge strategy before cleanup.
author: RAMitchell

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR introduces a variable-size reduction primitive (`collective::ReduceV`) and switches distributed quantile sketch merging from allgather/materialization to a reduce-merged byte payload that is then broadcast back to workers, aiming to reduce peak RSS and improve performance. **Changes:** - Add `collective::ReduceV` to support reductions over variable-length, trivially-copyable payloads with a custom merge function. - Update distributed quantile sketch combine to serialize per-feature numeric summaries into a byte payload, reduce-merge/prune them, then broadcast the merged payload for reconstruction. - Add tests for `ReduceV` and add benchmark-style distributed quantile “bench” gtests (currently running as part of the unit test suite). ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | tests/cpp/common/test_quantile.cc | Adds env-driven distributed quantile benchmark tests and helpers for parsing env vars. | | tests/cpp/collective/test_allreduce.cc | Adds a unit test that validates `ReduceV` behavior with variable-length vectors and different roots. | | src/common/quantile.cc | Implements the payload serialization/parsing and switches sketch merge to `ReduceV` + broadcast reconstruction. | | src/collective/allreduce.h | Adds the `ReduceV` implementation and convenience overloads for `Context`/`CommGroup`. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

title: Bump the github-actions group with 2 updates
body:
Bumps the github-actions group with 2 updates: [actions/upload-artifact](https://github.com/actions/upload-artifact) and [actions/download-artifact](https://github.com/actions/download-artifact). Updates `actions/upload-artifact` from 6.0.0 to 7.0.0
Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Add proxy integration test by @​Link- in actions/upload-artifact#754
  • Upgrade the module to ESM and bump dependencies by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#762
  • Support direct file uploads by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#764

New Contributors

  • @​Link- made their first contribution in actions/upload-artifact#754

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/upload-artifact/compare/v6...v7.0.0

Commits
  • bbbca2d Support direct file uploads (#764)
  • 589182c Upgrade the module to ESM and bump dependencies (#762)
  • 47309c9 Merge pull request #754 from actions/Link-/add-proxy-integration-tests
  • 02a8460 Add proxy integration test
  • See full diff in compare view

Updates `actions/download-artifact` from 7.0.0 to 8.0.0
Release notes

Sourced from actions/download-artifact's releases.

v8.0.0

v8 - What's new

Direct downloads

To support direct uploads in actions/upload-artifact, the action will no longer attempt to unzip all downloaded files. Instead, the action checks the Content-Type header ahead of unzipping and skips non-zipped files. Callers wishing to download a zipped file as-is can also set the new skip-decompress parameter to false.

Enforced checks (breaking)

A previous release introduced digest checks on the download. If a download hash didn't match the expected hash from the server, the action would log a warning. Callers can now configure the behavior on mismatch with the digest-mismatch parameter. To be secure by default, we are now defaulting the behavior to error which will fail the workflow run.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Don't attempt to un-zip non-zipped downloads by @​danwkennedy in actions/download-artifact#460
  • Add a setting to specify what to do on hash mismatch and default it to error by @​danwkennedy in actions/download-artifact#461

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/download-artifact/compare/v7...v8.0.0

Commits
  • 70fc10c Merge pull request #461 from actions/danwkennedy/digest-mismatch-behavior
  • f258da9 Add change docs
  • ccc058e Fix linting issues
  • bd7976b Add a setting to specify what to do on hash mismatch and default it to error
  • ac21fcf Merge pull request #460 from actions/danwkennedy/download-no-unzip
  • 15999bf Add note about package bumps
  • 974686e Bump the version to v8 and add release notes
  • fbe48b1 Update test names to make it clearer what they do
  • 96bf374 One more test fix
  • b8c4819 Fix skip decompress test
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

title: Reduce memory in GPU quantile sketch for external memory
body: Accumulation of exponentionally increasing numbers of sketch containers here is unecessary. Simply merging and pruning GK sketches for each batch maintains the error bound.
author: RAMitchell

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR optimizes memory usage in GPU quantile sketch generation for external memory by replacing the accumulation of exponentially growing sketch containers with a single container that is merged and pruned after each batch. This maintains the same error bounds while significantly reducing memory footprint. **Changes:** - Simplified the sketching algorithm from multi-level accumulation to single-container merge-and-prune approach - Removed streaming algorithm infrastructure (pair tracking, lazy initialization, capacity calculation) - Added `SetNumRows` method to `SketchContainer` for updating row count ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/data/quantile_dmatrix.cu | Replaced multi-level sketch accumulation with single container approach; removed streaming algorithm logic and simplified batch processing | | src/common/quantile.cuh | Added `SetNumRows` method to support updating the row count in the simplified algorithm | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: [mt] Unify the quantisers.
body:
- Support distributed training.
- Host device access. Host access is for single-target. We will migrate to device-only once we can unify the code base between single-target and multi-target more.

ref https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/9043
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR unifies the gradient quantiser implementation to support distributed training for multi-output models. The changes introduce a new `GradientQuantiserGroup` class that replaces the previous `MultiGradientQuantiser` and provides both device-side access (via `DeviceSpan()`) and host-side access (via `operator[]`) to quantisers. The implementation moves quantiser construction logic into a new `quantiser.cu` file and optimizes distributed training by batching multiple collective operations together.

**Changes:**
- Introduced unified `GradientQuantiserGroup` class replacing `MultiGradientQuantiser` with dual host/device access
- Moved quantiser implementation to new `quantiser.cu` file with optimized collective operations for distributed training
- Updated all quantiser access patterns from `.Quantizers()` to `.DeviceSpan()` for device access and `operator[]` for host access
- Modified distributed training check to allow distributed training for non-vector-leaf cases

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 10 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/tree/gpu_hist/quantiser.cuh | Defines new unified `GradientQuantiserGroup` class with host/device dual interface |
| src/tree/gpu_hist/quantiser.cu | New file containing quantiser implementation with batched collective operations for distributed training |
| src/tree/gpu_hist/histogram.cu | Removed quantiser implementation code (moved to quantiser.cu) |
| src/tree/updater_gpu_hist.cu | Updated single-target quantiser usage to use new `GradientQuantiserGroup` with `operator[]` for host access |
| src/tree/updater_gpu_hist.cuh | Updated multi-target quantiser usage and relaxed distributed training restriction to allow non-vector-leaf cases |

title: Move internal shap algorithms into separate namespace.
author: RAMitchell

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR refactors SHAP-related implementation details out of the CPU/GPU predictors into a new internal `xgboost::interpretability` namespace, aiming to reduce duplication and better isolate interpretability code paths.

**Changes:**
- Introduces new internal SHAP dispatch API (`interpretability::ShapValues`, `ShapInteractionValues`, `ApproxFeatureImportance`) with CPU and CUDA implementations.
- Refactors CPU/GPU predictors to delegate SHAP/interaction contribution computation to the new interpretability layer.
- Extracts shared CPU/GPU data access utilities into dedicated headers and updates SHAP tests to use the new entry points (with reduced runtime).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 13 out of 13 changed files in this pull request and generated 8 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `tests/cpp/predictor/test_shap.cc` | Updates SHAP tests to call new interpretability SHAP entry points and reduces dataset sizes/iters. |
| `src/predictor/interpretability/shap.h` | Adds internal SHAP dispatch header (CPU/CUDA routing). |
| `src/predictor/interpretability/shap.cc` | Adds CPU implementations for SHAP values, interactions, and approximate importance. |
| `src/predictor/interpretability/shap.cu` | Adds CUDA implementations for SHAP values/interactions using GPUTreeShap. |
| `src/predictor/gpu_predictor.cu` | Removes inlined GPU SHAP logic and delegates to interpretability SHAP functions; reuses extracted GPU accessors. |
| `src/predictor/gpu_data_accessor.cuh` | New shared GPU sparse/ellpack access helpers (used by GPU predictor and SHAP). |
| `src/predictor/data_accessor.h` | New shared CPU batch-to-FVec access helpers (used by CPU predictor and CPU SHAP). |
| `src/predictor/cpu_predictor.cc` | Removes inlined CPU SHAP logic and delegates to interpretability SHAP functions; uses extracted accessors. |
| `src/gbm/gbtree.h` | Formatting-only adjustments.

body: Close https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/12039
author: trivialfis

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR enhances the model serialization documentation by adding important security warnings about pickle usage and improving the overall clarity and organization of the content. It addresses issue #12039 regarding the security risks of loading pickled files from untrusted sources. **Changes:** - Added security warning about pickle not being safe for untrusted sources, including references to joblib and cloudpickle - Enhanced documentation about custom objective, metric, and callback functions - Reorganized and improved the section on loading pickled files from different XGBoost versions - Minor grammar corrections ("tree model" → "tree models") --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Philip Hyunsu Cho <chohyu01@cs.washington.edu>

title: Replace the global random engine.
body:
- Define the RNG in the `Context` class.
- Replace the global thread-local random engine with the new RNG in the `Context`.

Related:
- https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10354
- https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/12037
- https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/10324
- https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/6711
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR replaces the global thread-local random engine with a random engine stored in the `Context` class, improving encapsulation and making the RNG lifecycle more explicit and testable.

**Changes:**
- Moved `RandomEngine` type definition from `src/common/random.h` to `include/xgboost/context.h`
- Added `rng_` member and `Rng()` accessor method to the `Context` class
- Removed global `GlobalRandom()` function and its thread-local storage implementation
- Updated all call sites throughout the codebase to use `ctx->Rng()` or `ctx.Rng()` instead of `GlobalRandom()`
- Updated test files to use local `std::mt19937` instances instead of the global random engine
- Minor formatting improvements and error message clarifications

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 14 out of 14 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| include/xgboost/context.h | Added `RandomEngine` type alias, `Rng()` accessor method, and `rng_` mutable member to Context class |
| src/common/random.h | Removed `GlobalRandomEngine` and `GlobalRandom()` declarations; updated function signatures to use `RandomEngine*` |
| src/common/random.cu | Updated function signatures from `GlobalRandomEngine*` to `RandomEngine*` |
| src/common/common.cc | Removed `GlobalRandom()` implementation and thread-local storage |
| src/learner.cc | Updated RNG seeding to use `ctx_.Rng().seed()` instead of `GlobalRandom().seed()` |
| src/gbm/gbtree.cc | Replaced `GlobalRandom()` with `ctx_->Rng()` in DART implementation |
| src/tree/updater_colmaker.cc | Updated subsample random selection to use `ctx_->Rng()`; improved error message |
| src/tree/hist/sampler.cc | Updated parallel sampling to get seed from `ctx->Rng()` |
| src/tree/gpu_hist/sampler.cu | Updated GPU sampling to get seed from `ctx->Rng()` |
| src/tree/updater_gpu_hist.cu | Removed unused `GlobalRandom` inc

title: Use the pytest tmp path fixture
body:
This PR uses the `tmp_path` pytest fixture to replace the use of the `tempfile` module, in addition to some small cleanups for the tests. 

Lots of bulk changes are caused by pre-commit ruff.
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR modernizes test code by replacing `tempfile.TemporaryDirectory()` with pytest's built-in `tmp_path` fixture across multiple test files. The changes also include updating copyright years and minor formatting improvements.

**Changes:**
- Replaces `tempfile.TemporaryDirectory()` context managers with `tmp_path` fixture usage
- Updates copyright years to 2026 in some files
- Removes unnecessary `tempfile` and `os` imports
- Applies minor code formatting improvements (quotes, spacing)

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 15 out of 15 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| tests/python/test_openmp.py | Updates OpenMP tests to use `tmp_path` fixture |
| tests/python/test_model_io.py | Migrates model I/O tests to `tmp_path` fixture |
| tests/python/test_dmatrix.py | Updates DMatrix tests to use `tmp_path` fixture |
| tests/python/test_demos.py | Converts demo tests to use `tmp_path` fixture |
| tests/python/test_data_iterator.py | Updates data iterator tests to use `tmp_path` |
| tests/python/test_callback.py | Migrates callback tests to `tmp_path` fixture |
| tests/python/test_basic_models.py | Updates basic model tests to

Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v6.0.0

v6 - What's new

[!IMPORTANT] actions/upload-artifact@v6 now runs on Node.js 24 (runs.using: node24) and requires a minimum Actions Runner version of 2.327.1. If you are using self-hosted runners, ensure they are updated before upgrading.

Node.js 24

This release updates the runtime to Node.js 24. v5 had preliminary support for Node.js 24, however this action was by default still running on Node.js 20. Now this action by default will run on Node.js 24.

  • Some errors were made into annotations to make them more visible

  • The underlying container for the action is now hosted on GitHub Container Registry. There should be no functional changes.

title: [CI] Avoid push runs caused by dependabot.
body:
Only run tests on persistent branches.

related: https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/12034
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR updates GitHub Actions workflow configurations to prevent unnecessary CI runs triggered by dependabot pushes. The change restricts push event triggers to only fire on the `master` branch and branches matching the `release_*` pattern, while keeping pull_request triggers unrestricted.

**Changes:**
- Modified trigger configurations for 12 GitHub Actions workflows to restrict push events to persistent branches (`master` and `release_*`)
- Maintains pull_request triggers without restrictions, ensuring all PRs (including from dependabot) still run CI checks
- Aligns all workflows with the existing pattern used in scorecards.yml

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 12 out of 12 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| .github/workflows/main.yml | Restricted push triggers to master and release_* branches |

title: Enable categorical feature support by default.
body:
close https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/12014

I kept the XGBoost parameter to avoid a breaking change to the API, and users can still disable categorical feature support if they wish.
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR enables categorical feature support by default in XGBoost by changing the default value of the `enable_categorical` parameter from `False` to `True`. This aligns with the goal stated in issue #12014 to make categorical support the default behavior while maintaining backward compatibility by keeping the parameter available for users who wish to disable it.

**Changes:**
- Changed default value of `enable_categorical` from `False` to `True` in core DMatrix classes and sklearn estimators
- Removed explicit `enable_categorical=True` from test code throughout the codebase
- Removed redundant Python-side validation for categorical features with exact tree method (now handled in C++)
- Applied code formatting improvements including import ordering standardization

### Reviewed changes

| python-package/xgboost/sklearn.py | Changed default value of `enable_categorical` to `True` in sklearn estimator base class; removed redundant validation logic for exact tree method with categorical features; improved docstring formatting |

Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | python-package/xgboost/sklearn.py | Adds pylint suppression and minor docstring formatting change. | | python-package/xgboost/data.py | Replaces imports from `core` with `_c_api` / `_typing`; loosens some type annotations to avoid runtime imports. | | python-package/xgboost/dask/__init__.py | Adds pylint suppression. | | python-package/xgboost/core.py | Imports low-level C-API symbols from `_c_api`, relocates iterator helpers, and removes embedded C-API loading/utility code. | | python-package/xgboost/_typing.py | Adds `DataSplitMode` enum. | | python-package/xgboost/_c_api.py | New module containing the low-level ctypes bridge and library loading logic. |

commits.message: Update call sites to reflect moved functions

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR centralizes the mutex used to prevent concurrent “pull model to device / create tree views” operations by moving it from individual predictors into `gbm::GBTreeModel`, so all predictors share a single lock when constructing `GBTreeModelView`. **Changes:** - Remove per-predictor model mutexes from CPU/GPU predictors and stop passing them into `GBTreeModelView`. - Add a `mutable std::mutex` to `gbm::GBTreeModel` and lock it inside `GBTreeModelView` during view creation. - Minor formatting-only adjustments in `gpu_predictor.cu`. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `src/predictor/gpu_predictor.cu` | Stops passing a predictor-owned mutex into `DeviceModel`; removes predictor mutex member. | | `src/predictor/gbtree_view.h` | `GBTreeModelView` now locks via `model.Mutex()` instead of an injected mutex pointer. | | `src/predictor/cpu_predictor.cc` | Stops passing a predictor-owned mutex into `HostModel`; removes predictor mutex member. | | `src/gbm/gbtree_model.h` | Adds the shared mutex and a public accessor `Mutex()` used by `GBTreeModelView`. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

My preference is not to have the mutex in the "model" and keep the "model" minimal in terms of mutable states. The model itself is a large state that changes in a "commit" style (in the sense of a database transaction).

My original plan was to create a task queue, similar to Rust's mpsc crate (https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/sync/mpsc/), where one pushes model views and tasks into the queue in a message-passing style, thereby eliminating the need for traditional mutexes. But since I haven't been able to prioritize it, these are just empty words. I will leave it for you to decide.

The PR looks good to me.

title: Fix compilation with the latest CCCL.
body:
- Fix CMake script to pick up the `cuda-cccl` from conda.
- Replace the `thrust::tuple`, except when it's used by `zip_iterator`. Only thrust>=3.0 uses `cuda::std::tuple` in the `zip_iterator`.


CI log: https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/actions/runs/21935620994/job/63349074776?pr=12013

**todos**
- [x] Figure out what to do with the cpp lint false positive.
- [x] Revert the CI pipeline changes.
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes compilation issues with the latest CCCL (CUDA C++ Core Libraries) by migrating from `thrust::tuple` to `cuda::std::tuple` throughout the codebase, with the important exception that `thrust::tuple` must still be used with `thrust::make_zip_iterator` (only thrust>=3.0 uses `cuda::std::tuple` in zip_iterator).

**Changes:**
- Updated CMake configuration to better detect CCCL from conda environments by adding hints to `CUDAToolkit_TARGET_DIR`
- Replaced `thrust::tuple`, `thrust::make_tuple`, `thrust::get`, `thrust::tie`, and `thrust::pair` with `cuda::std::` equivalents throughout the codebase, except where used with `zip_iterator`
- Updated copyright years to 2026 across multiple files
- Removed cpp linter's `include_what_you_use` check to avoid false positives from new CCCL includes
- Various formatting improvements (spacing, alignment)

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 13 out of 13 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| CMakeLists.txt | Added CUDAToolkit_TARGET_DIR hints to find_package calls for better CCCL detection in conda environments |
| ops/script/lint_cpp.py | Removed include_what_you_use linter check to avoid false positives |
| tests/cpp/common/test_quantile.cu | Attempted to migrate tuple usage but incorrectly used cuda::std::tuple with zip_iterator (should use thrust::tuple) |
| src/tree/gpu_hist/evaluator.cu | Correctly migrated to cuda::std::tuple for SortPair usage |
| src/tree/gpu_hist/evaluate_splits.cuh | Correctly updated SortPair type definition to cuda::std::tuple |
| src/objective/lambdarank_obj.cu | Correctly migrated to cuda::std::tuple while keeping thrust::tuple for zip_iterator |
| src/metric/auc.cu | Correctly migrated to cuda::std::tuple and cuda::std::pair |
| src/data/ellpack_page.cu | Fixed double semicolon typo, added thrust/tuple.h include for zip_iterator

disable the cpp lint include check.

title: Add support for expectile regression.
body:
Hello! This PR adds expectile regression support. Expectile is a regression method based on an asymmetric squared error, [1], which gives a smooth alternative to quantiles regression. These are implemented in catboost :) 

I'm not extremely familiar with the xgboost codebase, so I did my best to get something working and running from python, and got some help from codex for extra bindings in other languages as well as just finding what else had to be done.

What's in there:
- Add `reg:expectileerror` objective and `expectile` metric with `expectile_alpha` (consistent naming across C++/R/Python).
- Initialize expectile base score using (weighted) mean. One can compute the global expectile instead, but this requires solving an iterative equation, and I wasn't sure if that's something that one wants in every context, so I thought it would be better to skip it.
- Update docs and R tests for multi‑alpha output naming and printed metadata.
- Added unit tests.

The current state is a mix of "it works on my machine" and "unit tests pass", but I'm sure I'm missing some things here and would love to get more guidance!

[1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Expectile which is not very nice to read, I find the formulation here easier to read https://epub.ub.uni-muenchen.de/31542/1/1471082x14561155.pdf
author: matthieubulte

reviews.body: Thank you for working on this, let's go through the math step by step, starting with the intercept estimation. ;-)

Can the expectiles cross each other? e.g. in the prediction of a given leaf? 

Would love to see a convergence test. You could boost a set of tree stumps and see that a range of expectiles converges to known correct values.
Very nice PR.

## Pull request overview

This PR adds expectile regression to XGBoost by introducing a new regression objective (`reg:expectileerror`) and a corresponding evaluation metric (`expectile`), along with cross-language parameter plumbing (`expectile_alpha`) and tests/docs updates.

**Changes:**
- Added `reg:expectileerror` objective and `expectile` metric with `expectile_alpha` support (including multi-alpha behavior).
- Added unit/integration tests across C++, Python, and R, plus distributed/federated test parameter setup.
- Updated user-facing docs and R bindings (params, printing metadata, and prediction column naming for multi-alpha).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 23 out of 23 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

| `src/objective/regression_obj.cu` | Implements `reg:expectileerror` objective, gradients, and base score initialization. |
| `src/metric/elementwise_metric.cu` | Implements `expectile` evaluation metric. |
| `src/common/expectile_loss_utils.h` | Adds shared parameter struct/validation for `expectile_alpha`. |
| `src/common/expectile_loss_utils.cc` | Registers `ExpectileLossParam`. |
| `src/common/quantile_loss_utils.h` | Minor formatting change (blank line). |
| `tests/cpp/objective/test_regression_obj_cpu.cc` | Hooks unified C++ objective tests for expectile. |
| `tests/cpp/objective/test_regression_obj.h` | Declares expectile objective test helpers. |
| `tests/cpp/objective/test_regression_obj.cc` | Adds C++ tests for expectile gradients, multi-alpha, and init estimation. |
| `tests/cpp/objective/test_objective.cc` | Ensures tests configure `expectile_alpha` when expectile objecti
Thank you for the work on the expectile objective. Please rebase your branch to master and see if the CI can pass.

title: [EM] Support mixed GPU models on SNMG.
body:
- Use UUID to query C2C info, to support mixed GPU models.
- Drop the full-host-cache path to utilize large GPU memory.
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This pull request adds support for mixed GPU models on Single Node Multi-GPU (SNMG) configurations by using GPU UUIDs instead of device indices for querying C2C (chip-to-chip) link information. The changes also drop the special optimization path for full-host-cache on devices with high C2C bandwidth (link count >= 10), allowing the adaptive cache to utilize larger GPU memory more uniformly across different GPU configurations.

**Changes:**
- Added centralized UUID utility functions (`GetUuid` and `PrintUuid`) in the `curt` namespace for CUDA device identification
- Refactored NCCL communication setup to use the new UUID utilities instead of local implementations
- Updated C2C link detection to query using UUID instead of fixed device index 0
- Removed special-case handling for devices with link count >= 10 in the adaptive cache logic
- Updated documentation to reflect the simplified adaptive cache behavior

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| tests/cpp/common/test_cuda_rt_utils.cu | Adds test for new UUID utility functions |
| src/data/batch_utils.cc | Removes special-case optimization for high C2C bandwidth devices |
| src/common/cuda_rt_utils.h | Declares new UUID utility functions |
| src/common/cuda_rt_utils.cc | Implements UUID query and printing functions |
| src/common/cuda_dr_utils.cc | Updates C2C detection to use current device UUID instead of device 0 |
| src/collective/comm.cu | Refactors to use centralized UUID utilities instead of local implementation |
| doc/tutorials/external_memory.rst | Updates documentation to reflect simplified adaptive cache behavior |
</details>






---

- Query C2C info via UUID.
- Drop the specialization for full C2C to support large GPUs.

title: [CI] Speed up pyspark tests
body:
Local runtime from  0:09:48 to ~0:04:47, 51% improvement

- Reused Spark sessions
- Reduced Spark resources
- Smaller CV dataset
- Dynamic expected values
- Lower estimators / smaller data multipliers
- Barrier uses defaultParallelism
- Shuffle partitions down to 4
author: RAMitchell

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR optimizes PySpark test execution by implementing session reuse, reducing Spark resources, and dynamically generating expected values. The changes reduce test runtime from approximately 10 minutes to 5 minutes (51% improvement). **Changes:** - Introduced shared Spark session mechanism with environment variable control - Reduced Spark cluster resources (workers, cores, partitions) and disabled UI - Changed test fixtures from function to module scope for better reuse - Replaced hardcoded expected values with dynamically computed predictions from XGBoost - Reduced dataset sizes and estimator counts while maintaining test coverage ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | tests/test_distributed/test_with_spark/utils.py | Added session reuse logic, reduced Spark resources (2 workers, 1 core each), lowered shuffle partitions to 4, disabled UI, made barrier operation dynamic | | tests/test_distributed/test_with_spark/test_spark_local_cluster.py | Changed fixtures to module scope, converted setUp to setUpClass, generated expected values dynamically from trained models, reduced data multiplier from 100 to 20, lowered n_estimators | | tests/test_distributed/test_with_spark/test_spark_local.py | Changed fixtures to module scope, converted setUp to setUpClass, generated expected values dynamically, reduced training dataset multiplier from 100 to 20, lowered n_estimators to 20 | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: [mt] Initial subsample implementation.
body:
This PR ports the single-target sampling to the vector leaf. I will follow up with a complete implementation of MVS.

- Add CPU implementation for gradient-based sampling.
- Define the sampler in `sampler.cu/cc` files as it's not exclusively about MVS.
- Support vector leaf. The sampling should be consistent between gradient types. But the scale can differ. As a result, the value gradient might be biased. I don't have a good solution at the moment and assume the dimension reduction algorithm can do a decent job at maintaining bias and variance.

Ref https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/9043

related:
- https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/3734
author: trivialfis

This PR implements subsample support for multi-target (vector leaf) trees by porting single-target sampling to the vector leaf implementation. The implementation adds both uniform and gradient-based row sampling strategies for multi-target models.

title: Enable threaded data initialization for CPU.
body: Extracted from https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11390 , modified to use the context instead. In addition, a new `ParallelForBlock` is created.
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR refactors gradient index construction on CPU to consistently use `Context`-based threading and introduces a new block-based parallel helper for initializing reference-counted buffers.

**Changes:**
- Update `GHistIndexMatrix` constructors and internal methods to take `Context` and use new `MakeFixedVecWithMalloc(ctx, ...)` for multi-threaded, malloc-backed buffers on CPU (and hook up corresponding call sites and tests).
- Refactor gradient-index page sources (`SparsePageDMatrix::GetGradientIndex` and `GradientIndexPageSource`) to accept a `Context` and construct `GHistIndexMatrix` with it, including for external-memory workflows.
- Add `ParallelForBlock` and a `Context`-aware `MakeFixedVecWithMalloc` in `ref_resource_view`, using block-parallel initialization, and wire GPU-side code to the new `ResizeIndex(ctx, ...)` API.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 11 out of 11 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `tests/cpp/tree/test_quantile_hist.cc` | Adjusts quantile histogram partitioner tests to use the new `GHistIndexMatrix` constructor taking `Context const*` instead of a raw thread count. |
| `tests/cpp/tree/hist/test_histogram.cc` | Updates external-memory histogram test to construct `GHistIndexMatrix` with `Context const*`, matching the new API. |
| `tests/cpp/data/test_sparse_page_dmatrix.cc` | Updates gradient index external-memory tests to use the `Context`-aware `GHistIndexMatrix` constructor. |
| `src/data/sparse_page_dmatrix.cc` | Refactors `GetGradientIndex` to construct `GradientIndexPageSource` with a `Context const*`, so downstream gradient-index construction can use `ctx->Threads()`. |
| `src/data/gradient_index_page_source.h` | Changes `GradientIndexPageSource` to accept `Context const*` and pass `ctx->Threads()` into `PageSourceIncMixIn`, preparing for `Context`-driven CPU threading.
Copilot encountered an error and was unable to review this pull request. You can try again by re-requesting a review.

Current pre-commit setup calls the lint_python.py script, which touches all files. Pre-commit is intended to run fast and only touch changed files.

@trivialfis @hcho3 in case we have not fully captured the exceptions in the old file, if you notice pre-commit touch files it shouldn't please just update their exceptions.

The old clang format doesn't recognize `// clang-format off` comment. I would like to be able to keep some blocks from being formatted.

title: Fix logistic with constant labels.
body: Close https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/11499
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This pull request fixes a bug where XGBoost 3.0+ crashes when training data contains all identical labels (0.0 or 1.0) with logistic objectives. The issue occurred because the computed base_score (mean of labels) would be exactly 0.0 or 1.0, which failed validation and caused errors when applying the logit transformation. **Changes:** - Modified `ProbToMargin` in `LogisticRegression` to bound base_score to `[kRtEps, 1-kRtEps]` before applying logit transformation - Updated `CheckIntercept` to accept boundary values 0.0 and 1.0 for degenerate cases - Added utility functions (`Min`, `Logit`) and updated `kRtEps` declaration for consistency ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/objective/regression_loss.h | Bounded base_score in ProbToMargin, relaxed CheckIntercept to accept 0.0 and 1.0, updated error messages | | src/common/math.h | Added Logit function to extract logit computation | | src/common/common.h | Added Min template function for device-compatible min operation | | include/xgboost/base.h | Made kRtEps explicitly inline and changed type from bst_float to float | | python-package/xgboost/testing/intercept.py | Added test for degenerate case with all-0.0 and all-1.0 labels | | tests/python/test_intercept.py | Added test case that calls run_logistic_degenerate for CPU | | tests/python-gpu/test_gpu_intercept.py | Added test case that calls run_logistic_degenerate for GPU |

title: Fix flaky dask test.
body:
The `test_n_workers` got flaky with the latest Dask. The PR uses `scatter` to control data placement instead of `submit` and `persist`.

For reviewers:

XGBoost uses the union of all workers obtained from the input data to train a model. This test creates a list of dask arrays residing on different workers, and checks whether XGBoost can obtain the union set. The test was flaky because the `submit` did not put the data in the expected worker from time to time.
author: trivialfis

reviews.body: This looks fine from a dask side (though `client.submit` *should* have achieved the same outcome).

commits.message:
Fix flaky dask test.

The `test_n_workers` got flaky with the latest Dask. The PR uses `scatter` to control data
placement instead of `submit`.

## Pull request overview

This pull request migrates CUDA Linux builds from using Docker via `docker_run.py` to GitHub Actions native container support. This simplifies the build pipeline by running scripts directly inside containers rather than orchestrating Docker from the host.

**Changes:**
- Refactored CUDA build scripts to run directly inside GHA containers, removing Docker orchestration code
- Split build and audit steps into separate jobs to use different containers (build container vs manylinux container)
- Merged cuda13.yml workflow into main.yml to consolidate CI workflows and share the ECR login step

title: [mt] Support loss guide.
body:
ref https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/9043

To support loss guide, we have to keep a buffer for the entire tree's weight and gradient sum. We might do some optimizations in the future, right now this is focusing on feature coverage.
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds support for the "lossguide" grow policy for multi-target (vector leaf) training in XGBoost. Previously, using lossguide with multi-target models would result in a fatal error.

**Changes:**
- Removed the fatal error check that prevented lossguide grow policy with multi-target training
- Refactored node weight and split sum storage to use persistent buffers indexed by node ID instead of batch position, enabling proper support for loss-guide policy where entries can remain in the priority queue across evaluation rounds
- Added comprehensive test coverage for both depthwise and lossguide grow policies across CPU and GPU implementations

| src/tree/gpu_hist/multi_evaluate_splits.cu | Updated evaluation logic to use node-indexed persistent buffers for weights and split sums; modified to pass max_nidx for dynamic allocation |
