## Pull request overview

This pull request unifies the histogram kernels for single-target and vector-leaf (multi-target) implementations by merging them into a single codebase and removing on-the-fly fixed point conversion. The goal is to simplify the code while maintaining performance.

**Changes:**
- Unified histogram kernel implementations for single-target and multi-target scenarios
- Moved gradient quantization (fixed-point conversion) earlier in the pipeline, before histogram building
- Removed redundant kernel instantiations and simplified the histogram builder interface

| src/tree/gpu_hist/quantiser.cuh | Changed CalcQuantizedGpairs to accept MatrixView instead of mutable pointer |
| src/tree/updater_gpu_hist.cu | Moved quantization earlier, updated histogram building to use pre-quantized gradients |

ref https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/9043
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This pull request implements reduced gradient support for the CPU histogram tree builder, extending the existing CUDA-only feature. The reduced gradient feature allows using a lower-dimensional gradient for tree structure finding while using the full gradient for leaf weight calculation, which is beneficial for training on high-dimensional multi-output problems.

**Changes:**
- Implements `ExpandTreeLeaf` method to calculate leaf weights using value gradient for CPU hist
- Adds helper functions (`ReduceToRows`, `CheckPartitionerLeaves`) for gradient aggregation and validation
- Updates gradient handling throughout the histogram building pipeline to support different gradient dimensions
- Adds tests and documentation for the reduced gradient feature on CPU
- Includes a working example demonstrating the feature with both mean and SVD-based gradient reduction

title: Implement vector load for GPU compressed buffer.
body:
~Stacked on https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11894~

Mainly to improve performance for the vector leaf.

- Extract the byteswap implementation, add nvcc and msvc support.
- Use vector load in the compressed iterator.

*todos*
- [x] Increase buffer padding.
- [x] Test double iterator
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR implements vector load optimizations for GPU compressed buffers to improve performance for vector leaf operations. The changes introduce a new byte swap utility header, optimize the compressed iterator with vector loads, and increase buffer padding from 4 to 8 bytes to accommodate the new loading strategy. Key changes: - Refactored ByteSwap functionality into a new platform-agnostic header (`include/xgboost/byteswap.h`) - Optimized `CompressedIterator::operator[]` to use vector loads via new `Load64u`, `AlignDown`, and `MakePaddedPtr` helper functions - Increased buffer padding from 4 to 8 bytes to support aligned vector loads ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `include/xgboost/byteswap.h` | New header providing platform-specific ByteSwap implementations (CUDA, GLIBC, MSVC, fallback) | | `include/xgboost/json_io.h` | Refactored to use new ByteSwap header instead of local implementation | | `src/common/compressed_iterator.h` | Added vector load optimization with alignment helpers; increased padding to 8 bytes; improved comments | | `src/data/ellpack_page.cu` | Added include for compressed_iterator.h | | `tests/cpp/common/test_compressed_iterator.cc` | Updated test assertions to reflect new buffer size calculations with increased padding |

This PR adds basic regularization support for multi-target trees in XGBoost, enabling l1/l2 regularization, min_split_loss (gamma), and max_delta_step parameters. It also makes split gradients optional for tree objectives and includes several code cleanups.

**Key Changes:**
- Implemented regularization parameters (lambda, alpha, gamma, max_delta_step) for multi-target tree training across CPU and GPU updaters
- Modified Python API to allow tree objectives to optionally return None for split gradients, falling back to value gradients

title: [mt] Use target-major histogram layout.
body:
- Pre-calculate the int64 gradient and use a f-major layout (target-major). This sacrifices memory usage to get better performance for GPUs with low f64 capacity. We might want to make it optional in the future, or enable batched training for the gradient.
- Use a target-major layout for the histogram, similar to the CPU implementation.

The PR significantly improves the vector-leaf performance, especially for a large number of targets. We are getting close to a point where we can unify the histogram kernels by replacing the existing single-target version with the vector leaf version. I will do this once the overall vector leaf implementation is mature enough.

ref https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/9043
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR refactors the multi-target histogram implementation to use a target-major (F-major) layout instead of calculating gradients on-the-fly. The changes pre-calculate int64 gradients and reorganize histogram memory layout to improve vector-leaf performance, bringing it closer to unification with the single-target histogram kernel.

Key changes:
- Pre-calculate quantized int64 gradients before histogram building
- Remove `n_targets` parameter from `FeatureGroups` constructor (now handles single-target layout)
- Update histogram kernel to use target-major layout with separate blocks per target

title: [mt] Assign a thread for each target when building the histogram.
body:
~Stacked on https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11883~

This PR optimizes the histogram kernel for vector leaf. It provides a launch bound for the multi-target histogram kernel, and uses one thread for each target gradient. In addition, it tunes the occupancy by reducing register pressure and provides different policies for shared memory usage.

ref https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/9043
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR optimizes the histogram kernel for multi-target (vector leaf) training on GPU. It implements a thread-per-target approach with launch bounds and adds column sampling support for multi-target models.

Key changes:
- Assigns one thread per target gradient when building histograms, improving parallelism
- Adds launch bounds and architecture-specific tuning for the multi-target histogram kernel
- Implements column sampling and feature interaction constraints for multi-target GPU training
- Optimizes memory access patterns and reduces register pressure

title: [R-package] Fix off-by-one bug: nrounds=0 resulted in 2 iterations
body:
This fixes issue #11854.

The Bug ->In R, the expression 1:0 evaluates to c(1, 0). When nrounds=0, the training loop begin_iteration:end_iteration was executing twice instead of zero times.

Fix:
->Replaced the loop range with seq(from = begin_iteration, length.out = nrounds) to correctly handle the zero case.
->Added if (nrounds == 0) iteration <- end_iteration to ensure the iteration variable exists for downstream callbacks (specifically cb_outputs), preventing an "object not found" error when the loop is skipped.
author: Sanidhyavijay24

reviews.body:
Thank you for the fix, @Sanidhyavijay24 ! Looks good to me.

cc @david-cortes I would like to separate the issue of callbacks with this fix, the Python package can use some more tests as well. I tested the empty model primarily for weird distributed environments and the tests are not as comprehensive.

We will backport this PR to the 3.1 branch for the next CRAN patch release.
LGTM, just a few comments.

title: [CI] Set up a nightly pipeline to test XGBoost with latest CCCL/RMM
body:
Example CI run: https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/actions/runs/20156973678, where the pipeline detects breakage with CCCL 3.2.0 RC1 and RMM 26.02 nightly.

There are two jobs in the pipeline:
* `test-latest-cccl` builds XGBoost with the latest version of CCCL (RC included). RMM is turned off for this job. (This is to avoid incompatibilities between latest CCCL and latest RMM.)
* `test-latest-rmm` builds XGBoost with the latest development version of RMM. The job uses the same CCCL version as rest of Rapids.

cc @csadorf @jakirkham @bdice 

The pipeline will run on a nightly basis. In addition, it's also possible to manually trigger the pipeline via the workflow dispatch.

@trivialfis Eventually this testing will be moved to CCCL and cuML for the sake of better visibility. For now, we host it in the XGBoost repo. It is set up as a nightly pipeline so that it doesn't block daily development.

author: trivialfis

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR implements prediction cache support for multi-target trees in the GPU histogram tree builder. The prediction cache stores the final node positions for training samples, enabling faster prediction updates without having to traverse the tree for each prediction. ### Key Changes - Added `positions_` member variable to cache node indices for each sample in multi-target training - Implemented `FinalizePosition` method to compute and store final leaf positions after tree construction - Implemented `UpdatePredictionCache` method to update predictions using cached positions - Refactored histogram allocation logic by changing `force_global` flag from true to false for better memory efficiency ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/tree/updater_gpu_hist.cuh | Added prediction cache infrastructure for multi-target trees including positions storage, FinalizePosition and UpdatePredictionCache methods | | src/tree/updater_gpu_hist.cu | Updated UpdatePredictionCache to support both single-target and multi-target trees, improved variable naming consistency (d_matrix → d_acc), and enhanced code comments | | src/tree/gpu_hist/histogram.cuh | Refactored subtraction logic by extracting CanSubtract helper method for improved code organization | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR implements shared memory histogram support for multi-target learning in the GPU histogram builder. The key objective is to optimize histogram computation by utilizing shared memory where possible and enabling histogram building across multiple tree nodes simultaneously.

**Key Changes:**
- Unified histogram kernel implementation that supports both shared and global memory for multi-target scenarios
- Extended API to build histograms for multiple nodes in a single call, improving parallelization
- Added comprehensive parameterized tests to validate various configurations (sample sizes, features, targets)

title: Add Intel as Sponsor
body: Intel donated $ 3600 on December 2, which qualifies for annual sponsorship.
author: hcho3

title: [R] Remove calls to R's global RNG
body:
At some point, xgboost was using R's random number generators from C++, but those calls were removed in favor of pre-drawing a seed from the  R side to pass to C++ random number generators when needed.

But some calls to the RNG state were still left in the codebase. This PR removes them since they aren't needed anymore.

title: [R] Fix memory safety issues
body:
Fixes a few issues detected by rchk.

@trivialfis You'll likely be asked to resubmit a fixed version of xgboost to CRAN after a few days. This PR will need to be backported if there isn't any 3.2 release by then. There might be more required changes from other checks, so let's wait for their email before resubmitting.
author: david-cortes

title: Make block_size of BuildHistKernel adaptive
body:
Current version of xgboost utilizes fixed block_size = 256 for hist building.

This PR make this value an adaptive function of model parameters and CPU cache size. The change is important mostly for ColsWiseBuildHistKernel and demonstrates up to 2x speed-up for epsilon dataset.

This PR updates the usage of RMM for compatibility with 25.12. The main changes are migrations in header locations and changes in the allocation API to align with CCCL's memory resource design.

This change requires RMM 25.12 or higher, so we may need some updates in the CI system.

title: [mt] Implement histogram subtraction.
body: ref https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/9043
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR implements histogram subtraction for multi-target GPU histogram-based tree building. The implementation allows computing histograms for sibling nodes more efficiently by subtracting one histogram from the parent instead of building both from scratch.

**Key changes:**
- Introduces a template-based `AssignNodes` function to determine which child node histograms to build vs. compute by subtraction
- Refactors `CalcRootSum` from a member function to a non-member function for better reusability
- Adds `ReduceHist` method that performs histogram subtraction and builds only necessary histograms

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/tree/updater_gpu_hist.cuh | Adds template-based `AssignNodes` and non-member `CalcRootSum`, implements `ReduceHist` for histogram subtraction, updates `PartitionAndBuildHist` to use subtraction optimization |
| src/tree/updater_gpu_hist.cu | Removes old non-template `AssignNodes` and updates call site to use new template version |
| src/tree/gpu_hist/multi_evaluate_splits.cuh | Adds clarifying comments for buffer member variables |
| src/tree/gpu_hist/multi_evaluate_splits.cu | Removes unused variable, refactors scan buffer initialization, adds first Hessian tracking for node size estimation |
| src/tree/gpu_hist/expand_entry.cuh | Adds `left_fst_hess` and `right_fst_hess` fields with `UpdateFirstHessian` method to support histogram subtraction decisions |
| src/tree/gpu_hist/leaf_sum.cu | Updates comment to clarify thrust iterator compatibility |
| src/data/batch_utils.h | Removes `CachePageBytesIsAuto` inline function definition |
| src/data/batch_utils.cc | Moves `CachePageBytesIsAuto` implementation from header to source file with updated logic |
</details>

title: [mt] Split up the tree model weights, support extmem.
body:
- Store base weight and leaf weight differently, as the size of base weight can be smaller.
- Basic tests for external memory.

Ref https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/9043 .

- [x] MemCostBytes
- [x] Copy
- [x] Document about the storage format.
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR refactors the multi-target tree model to separate internal base weights from leaf output weights. The key motivation is that base weights can be smaller when using reduced gradients, while leaf weights always need to be full-size for predictions.

Key changes:
- Split `weights_` into `weights_` (internal base weights) and `leaf_weights_` (output weights)
- Reuse the `right_` child field to store leaf weight indices for leaf nodes
- Add `SetLeaves()` method to copy base weights to leaf weights for non-reduced trees

### Reviewed Changes

title: Fix ordering of Python callbacks.

title: [R] More informative error when passing factors to DMatrix
body: DMatrix assumes that `label` is passed as an already-encoded vector or matrix. This PR makes it error out early when passing a `factor` type which is not supported, with an informative message, instead of erroring out later with some error that's harder to understand.

title: Cleanup linalg kernels.
body:
- Split up two different transform kernels.
- Handle the dispatching in the `linalg_op.h` header instead of the client code.
- Use thrust transform for transform kernels. Helps with memory IO.

The objective is to handle more linalg related operations like the ones used in https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11786 .

reviews.body:

This PR refactors the linalg kernel infrastructure by splitting transformation kernels into more specialized functions and moving device dispatching logic from client code into the `linalg_op.h` header. The refactoring also leverages thrust transform for better memory I/O performance in CUDA kernels.

Key changes:
- Split `ElementWiseTransformHost/Device` into `TransformIdxKernel` and `TransformKernel` with clearer semantics
- Centralized device dispatching in `linalg_op.h` using `Context::DispatchDevice`
- Implemented thrust-based transform for contiguous memory in CUDA kernels

title: [mt] Split up gradient types for the GPU hist.
body:
- Support using reduced gradient for tree structure exploration.

The PR adds a gradient container with two different gradient types: one for tree splits and the other for leaf values. This is an optimization for vector-leaf to reduce the overhead of finding the tree structure. Currently, the interface is exposed through a custom objective function by creating a specialized tree objective.

An alternative approach would be to decouple the dimension reduction function from the objective function by introducing an additional parameter, such as `grad_reducer`. This is actually the first approach I tried.

I don't have a strong opinion on which one we should choose. The objective approach facilitates associating the reduced gradient with the prediction and label more easily, but may be less modularized.

ref https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/9043
author: trivialfis

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR introduces support for reduced gradients in multi-target tree construction, enabling different gradients for split evaluation versus leaf value calculation. This is part of an experimental feature for improving multi-target learning.

Key changes:
- Introduces a new `GradientContainer` struct to hold both split gradients and optional value gradients
- Adds experimental `TreeObjective` Python class with `split_grad` method for custom gradient reduction
- Refactors tree updaters and booster code to use `GradientContainer` instead of raw gradient matrices
- Renames `SetLeaf` to `SetRoot` for multi-target trees to clarify semantics

title: Fix ranking tests with weight norm.

Fix ranking tests with weight norm.

The test might fail if the objective cache is renewed. This PR makes sure the objective
and its cache is recreated for each test case, and multiple the normalization factor into
the expected results.

title: Multi target memory reallocation optimization
body:
This PR extends the memory reallocation optimization introduced in #11112 to `MultiTargetHistBuilder`.
In addition, this pr also adds a `resize()` call to both `MultiTargetHistBuilder` and `HistUpdater` as safeguards in situations when number of pages decreases. 

I did a quick benchmark using a few datasets from https://www.csie.ntu.edu.tw/~cjlin/libsvmtools/datasets/multilabel.html that aren't too small while I could fit in my ec2 instance (c7i.24xl), and the changes seems consistent:

|                      | speed up |
| :------------------- | :------- |
| BlogCatalog          | 1.01     |
| Flickr               | 1.17     |
| siam-competition2007 | 1.00     |
| mediamill            | 1.10     |
| YouTube              | 1.04     |
author: siqi-he

title: optimize CPU inference with Array-Based Tree Traversal
body:
This PR introduces optimization for CPU inference. For each tree, the top N levels are transformed into a compact array-based layout. This allows for a branchless node indexing rule: idx = 2 * idx + int(val < split_cond). To minimize memory overhead, this transformation from the standard tree structure to the array layout is performed on-the-fly for each block of data being processed.  Even with this additional calculations, improved data locality in the cache-friendly array layout leads to inference speed up to ~2x (x1.4 on average). 
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4508af44-0b98-42a9-9fec-ab2b7b4fc25e)
author: razdoburdin

reviews.body:
Cosmetic changes.

The next possible step would be to convert the trees into array-based representation only once, and not to do it for each block of data.
Still trying to understand the code, will give it a try later. In the meanwhile, could you please craft some specific unittests for the new inference algorithm?

I'm still trying to understand the code, in the meantime, let me do some refactoring in this and the next week to accommodate the new optimization. We need a better structure to handle all these:
- Predict with scalar leaf.
- Predict with vector leaf.
- Array predict with scalar leaf.
- Array predict with vector leaf.
- Column split with scalar leaf.

I think I will split up the CPU predictor into multiple pieces.

Thank you for the excellent optimization!

I can understand the code (mostly), and it should be cleaner after merging into the regtree. I will merge this PR once the CI is green.

title: Small refactor for the GPU predictor.
body: This is to help the larger one: https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11730 . The objective is to phase out the `DeviceModel` and the `TreeView` that are specific to the GPU predictor. We will go through multiple PRs to achieve that as components are too interconnected.
author: trivialfis

reviews.body: ## Pull Request Overview Refactors GPU predictor code to unify kernel launch configuration and data path handling, moving toward phasing out GPU-specific DeviceModel/TreeView usage. Key updates include centralizing launch configuration, adding dense/sparse dispatch helpers, and modernizing CUDA utilities usage. - Introduces LaunchConfig to encapsulate kernel launch parameters and per-batch iteration for both SparsePage and Ellpack. - Replaces ad-hoc lambda transformations with cuda::proclaim_return_type and new iterator utilities. - Adds SparsePageView host-side constructor; threads through number of features to kernels. ### Reviewed Changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/predictor/gpu_predictor.cu | Refactors kernel launch flow, adds LaunchConfig, updates SHAP paths, switches to cuda utilities, and passes n_features explicitly. | | src/data/ellpack_page.cuh | Exposes NumRows() on EllpackAccessorImpl to support unified batching utilities. | --- **Tip:** Customize your code reviews with copilot-instructions.md. Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1) **src/predictor/gpu_predictor.cu:1175** * LaunchPredict uses DeviceModel.n_features (and cat_enc) to build the LaunchConfig, but d_model is not initialized for the multi-target branch, leaving n_features at 0 and cat_enc null. This can result in incorrect n_features_ in ForEachBatch (e.g., SparsePageView constructed with 0 features) and skipping category recoding. Initialize the required fields even in the multi-target case, e.g., set d_model.n_features = model.learner_model_param->num_feature; and d_model.cat_enc = model.Cats(); before calling LaunchPredict, or modify LaunchPredict to accept n_features and cat_enc (from the host model) instead of taking them from DeviceModel. ``` DeviceModel d_model; if (!model.trees[tree_begin]->IsMultiTarget()) { d_model.Init(model, tree_begin, tree_end, ctx_->Device()); } ```

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR implements vector intercept functionality by extracting fit intercept procedures from the learner and implementing vector transform in objectives. The changes enable support for vector-based base scores and intercepts in multi-class and multi-target models.

Key changes:
- Transform `ProbToMargin` from scalar to vector operations to support multi-output models
- Refactor intercept initialization and model parameter handling for vector base scores
- Add CUDA implementations for vector operations and histogram computations

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 29 out of 29 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.

| File | Description |

| tests/cpp/common/test_algorithm.cu | Adds test for SmallHistogram CUDA implementation |

| src/objective/init_estimation.cc | Simplifies intercept fitting by removing unnecessary mean calculation |
| src/objective/aft_obj.cu | Updates

## Pull Request Overview This PR implements vector support for the intercept (base_score) parameter in XGBoost. Previously, the intercept was limited to a scalar value, but this change allows it to be a vector for multi-class and multi-target scenarios. The implementation extracts base_score fitting procedures from the learner into objective functions and updates the parameter handling system to support array values. - Migrates from scalar to vector representation of base_score throughout the codebase - Implements multi-class intercept estimation using histogram aggregation for classification - Updates parameter serialization to use JSON arrays instead of scalars for base_score ### Reviewed Changes Copilot reviewed 72 out of 73 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR cleans up the predictor module by refactoring common functionality and improving code organization. The main goal is to reduce code duplication and create shared utilities for predictor implementations.

- Extracts common validation logic into a reusable utility function
- Introduces a ThreadTmp class to better manage thread-local feature vector storage

**src/predictor/gpu_predictor.cu:958**

```
    auto it = max_shared.find(device.ordinal);
```
</details>

## Pull Request Overview

This PR adds support for leaf prediction with QuantileDMatrix (QDM) on CPU, which was previously only supported on GPU. The implementation introduces thread-local temporary storage utilities and unifies validation checks for proxy DMatrix operations across both CPU and GPU predictors.

Key changes:
- Added `ThreadTmp` class to manage thread-local feature vector storage
- Created unified `CheckProxyDMatrix` utility for validating proxy DMatrix operations  
- Refactored CPU predictor to use policy-based dispatch similar to GPU implementation
- Extended test coverage to validate leaf prediction works with both DMatrix and QuantileDMatrix types

This pull request adds support for training continuation in XGBoost's Dask interface, specifically focusing on handling categorical encoding consistency across different datasets. The changes enable automatic re-coding of categorical features when continuing training with different datasets that may have different category encodings.

## Pull Request Overview

This PR exposes the categories container into Python by creating a new Python class wrapper and making pyarrow optional for categorical data handling. The changes refactor the categories API to return a container object instead of direct pyarrow arrays, while maintaining backwards compatibility through the `export_to_arrow` parameter.

Key changes:
- Refactor C API to use opaque handles for category containers with separate memory management
- Add a new Python `Categories` class that wraps the C++ container and provides controlled access to arrow exports
- Make pyarrow dependency optional by allowing category containers to be created without immediate arrow conversion

title: Add support for building xgboost wheels on Win-ARM64
body:
PR Description:

- The adoption of Windows on ARM (WoA) devices is steadily increasing, yet many Python wheels are still not available for this platform.
- GitHub Actions now offer native CI runners for Windows on ARM devices (windows-11-arm), enabling automated builds and testing.
- Currently, official XGBoost Python wheels are not provided for Windows ARM64.
- This PR introduces support for building XGBoost wheels on Windows ARM64, improving accessibility for developers and end users on this emerging platform.

This PR improves NUMA topology detection and cross-socket access checking by implementing more rigorous validation to avoid false positives. The changes move NUMA-related functionality to a dedicated location and enhance the detection logic to check for both CPU and normal memory availability before flagging potential issues.

Key changes:
- Moved `GetCpuNuma` function from threading utilities to the NUMA topology module for better organization

## Pull Request Overview

This PR implements more rigorous checks for potential cross-socket memory access in NUMA systems, focusing on reducing false positives and improving validation. The changes enhance NUMA topology detection by adding functions to check for CPU and normal memory nodes, refactor version checking utilities, and improve error handling for host cache operations.

- Move CPU NUMA detection functions from threading utilities to NUMA topology module

title: Fix compilation of Xgboost on WoA
body:
PR Description:

- The PR fixes compilation of Xgboost library on WoA(Windows on ARM).
- Building xgboost from source on native WoA device fails while including bitfield.h header due to x64 based intrinsic function call _tzcnt_u32(v).
- The PR adds a macro to call _tzcnt_u32(v) only for x64 based MSVC-like compilers
- This leads to successful build of xgboost on WoA using MSVC toolchain.

reviews.body: Thank you for the fix! Will merge once we can restore the CI.

title: Adding `enable_categorical` to .apply method
body:
Currently, `XGBModel` can be trained with `enable_categorical=True` when the input data fields have `categorical` type when appropriate. However, the `.apply` method will throw when trying to retrieve leaf values.



Example
```python
import xgboost as xgb
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

model = xgb.XGBClassifier(enable_categorical = True)

arr = np.random.rand(5,5)

df = pd.DataFrame(arr)

df['test']= ['one', 'two','three', 'four', 'five']


df = df.convert_dtypes()

# convert string to categorical
df['test'] = df['test'].astype('category')

model.fit(df, [0,1,2,3,4]) # works


model.apply(df) # fails
```

Produces

```
Exception has occurred: ValueError
DataFrame.dtypes for data must be int, float, bool or category. When categorical type is supplied, the experimental DMatrix parameter`enable_categorical` must be set to `True`.  Invalid columns:test: category
KeyError: 'category'

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

  File "/path/to/python/file/test.py", line 22, in <module>
    model.apply(df)
ValueError: DataFrame.dtypes for data must be int, float, bool or category. When categorical type is supplied, the experimental DMatrix parameter`enable_categorical` must be set to `True`.  Invalid columns:test: category
```

title: [CI] [WheelNext] Build variant wheels
body:
WheelNext is a proposal to improve Python wheels to better support specialized hardware and reduce the size of individual wheels. In particular, we are keen to adopt [the Wheel Variant proposal](https://wheelnext.dev/proposals/pepxxx_wheel_variant_support/). Also see [the presentation](https://wheelnext.dev/summits/2025_03/assets/WheelNext%20Community%20Summit%20-%2012%20-%20Wheel%20Variants.pdf).

Use the prototype in https://github.com/wheelnext/pep_xxx_wheel_variants to build a variant wheel. A variant wheel is just like a regular wheel, but with extra variant metadata like `nvidia :: cuda :: 12`.

title: Fix static linking C++ libraries on macOS
body:
Currently, if OpenMP is enabled and you're on macOS, the `patch_openmp_path_macos(...)` CMake helper function is called to adjust the RPATH in `libxgboost.dylib` to allow usage of other OpenMP libraries at install time, as described here: https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/blob/68182c26503083a17bf06ecc530cf09c5d0dd942/cmake/FindOpenMPMacOS.cmake#L29-L35

Problem is, when you are compiling xgboost statically via `BUILD_STATIC_LIB=ON`, the expected `.dylib` file will not exist, and the call to `install_name_tool` will error.

This PR adds an extra check to where the call to `patch_openmp_path_macos(...)` is made to simply not do that if `BUILD_STATIC_LIB` is `ON`.  Note that I did _not_ apply this to the JVM-bindings version of the call, because xgboost doesn't support static builds for JVM bindings (guarded by this: https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/blob/68182c26503083a17bf06ecc530cf09c5d0dd942/CMakeLists.txt#L169-L170).

Note: there seems to be a build failure for docs on JVM packages - that seems unrelated to this change, as it's also happening on `master`
author: danpat

commits.message:
Update CMakeLists.txt

When building static libraries on macOS, we don't need to patch any rpaths - there are none at play.  The current function tries to update the `libxgboost.dylib` always, which fails when `BUILD_STATIC_LIB` is set to `ON`.

title: Fixing a bug in the Spark fit-method when passing in None for certain parameters
body:
When working with the Spark Classifier class we are running into a small bug when trying to fit a model where we pass in `None` as the argument for `weight_col`. We worked around by passing in an empty string, but I figured I'd fix the issue here.

The issue is in  `_col_is_defined_not_empty` method in the python-package/xgboost/spark/core.py file.

When `weight_col = None`:
- `self.isDefined(param)` returns `True` (the parameter is set)
- `self.getOrDefault(param)` returns `None`
- `None != ""` evaluates to `True`

So the method incorrectly returns `True`, causing the code to try to select a column with name `None`

With this fix, the issue is resolved. 

Please let me know if you need anything else for me to do. I didn't add a test for this edge case because the overall method didn't have one either.
author: jborchma

reviews.body: Thank you for the fix!

commits.message: added fix for none parameter column name issue

title: Make xgboost.testing compatible with scikit-learn 1.7
body:
Starting with scikit-learn 1.7, the `sklearn.utils.stats._weighted_percentile` method changed its function signature. Instead of `percentile` parameter, it now expects `percentile_rank`. See https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/pull/29034. Such incompatible changes are expected, as we are using an internal method.

This should fix errors like
```
tree_method = 'hist', weighted = True, device = 'cpu'

    def run_adaptive(tree_method: str, weighted: bool, device: Device) -> None:
        """Test for adaptive trees."""
        rng = np.random.RandomState(1994)
        from sklearn.datasets import make_regression
        from sklearn.utils import stats
    
        n_samples = 256
        X, y = make_regression(  # pylint: disable=unbalanced-tuple-unpacking
            n_samples, 16, random_state=rng
        )
        if weighted:
            w = rng.normal(size=n_samples)
            w -= w.min()
            Xy = xgb.DMatrix(X, y, weight=w)
>           base_score = stats._weighted_percentile(  # pylint: disable=protected-access
                y, w, percentile=50
            )
E           TypeError: _weighted_percentile() got an unexpected keyword argument 'percentile'

/home/runner/.local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/xgboost/testing/updater.py:665: TypeError
```
(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/actions/runs/15484734790/job/43597489571)

This PR implements a double-buffer design for Ellpack pages so that external memory can leverage distinct host and device caches with a variant‐based accessor. title: rename param.hh/cc to hist_param.hh/cc to fix xcode build
body:
When building object library `objxgboost` with xcode, for example in the context of an iOS library/app, xcode is generating all object files in the same place, regardless of the actual path.
This creates collisions when two compilation units have the same name, for example
- `src/tree/hist/param.cc`
- `src/tree/param.cc`

Generate the following object files, notice the appended hashes only for files that have collisions:
```
./build/objxgboost.build/Debug-iphoneos/Objects-normal/arm64/in_memory_handler.o
./build/objxgboost.build/Debug-iphoneos/Objects-normal/arm64/timer.o
./build/objxgboost.build/Debug-iphoneos/Objects-normal/arm64/ellpack_page.o
./build/objxgboost.build/Debug-iphoneos/Objects-normal/arm64/comm_group.o
./build/objxgboost.build/Debug-iphoneos/Objects-normal/arm64/param-20b7432b394539ccbe53282f1bfc13b8.o
./build/objxgboost.build/Debug-iphoneos/Objects-normal/arm64/param-b1b2b65c7e82fe30091c637960712a86.o
```

When linking everything together, the linker cannot find the original file and reports the following error:
```
error: /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Toolchains/XcodeDefault.xctoolchain/usr/bin/libtool: can't open file: build/objxgboost.build/Debug-iphoneos/Objects-normal/arm64/param.o (No such file or directory)
```

This seems to happen only for xcode generator I've found online some issues related to xcode generator not working well with object libraries, for example:
- https://gitlab.kitware.com/cmake/cmake/-/issues/22415
- https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/issues/15369

The immediate fix is to just rename files that have the same name in order to avoid collisions when creating object files
- `src/tree/hist/param.cc` → `src/tree/hist/hist_param.cc`
- `src/tree/hist/param.h` → `src/tree/hist/hist_param.h`
author: gamante91

commits.message: rename param.hh/cc to hist_param.hh/cc to fix xcode build

title: [EM] Optimization for deep trees.
body:
- Decouple the row partition batch size from the driver batch size. This will allow us to process more nodes for each data batch.
- Pick a heuristic to use ATS instead of data copy to handle cases where we have a large number of small nodes.
- Make sure a new page that happens to be the last is placed on the host.

The optimization is specifically for GPU external memory. We need some kernel fusion to improve in-core training further.

title: Use GPU AllreduceV for distributed quantile sketch exchange
body:
## Summary

This changes the distributed GPU quantile sketch exchange from an allgather-style pattern to a GPU `AllreduceV` path built on the existing collective interfaces.

The new implementation packs the sketch into a raw byte payload, performs a variable-size reduction over NCCL, and materializes the reduced sketch back into `SketchContainer`. The reduction uses a tree of point-to-point edges for the reduce phase and the existing broadcast path for the final fan-out.

## Motivation

The previous allgather-style exchange causes communicated bytes and temporary communication storage to grow poorly with worker count because every rank materializes peer payloads.

A tree-shaped reduction keeps the communication pattern closer to the CPU `AllreduceV` path and avoids that replication on the NCCL fast path.

## Design

The main pieces are:
- a GPU `AllreduceV` API aligned with the existing collective API shape in `src/collective/allreduce.h`
- a private CUDA implementation in `src/collective/allreduce_v.cuh`
- quantile-side packing/unpacking in `src/common/quantile.cu`

The current NCCL implementation:
- uses existing `Comm::Chan(peer)` wrappers for tree `Send`/`Recv` edges
- uses the existing broadcast wrapper for the final dissemination
- preserves upstream nonblocking NCCL communicator init
- explicitly waits for communicator progress after p2p edges so the tree is stable with nonblocking NCCL communicators

## Main Changes

- Add GPU `AllreduceV` support to the collective layer.
- Route distributed GPU quantile sketch exchange through that helper instead of the old allgather-style path.
- Keep the quantile reduction logic local to `quantile.cu` by packing the sketch as bytes and reducing with merge + prune semantics.
- Add focused tests for the GPU `AllreduceV` tree shape and repeated-call stability.

## Testing

Ran:
- `MGPUAllreduceVTest.SimpleTree`
- `MGPUAllreduceVTest.RepeatedTree`
- `MGPUAllreduceTest.Timeout`
- `GPUQuantile.Prune`
- `GPUQuantile.MergeBasic`
- `GPUQuantile.MultiMerge`
- `MGPUQuantileTest.AllReduceBasic`

## Follow-up

- Replace the temporary relaxed quantile cut-value tolerance with a check based on sketch/rank guarantees.
- Decide how `AllreduceV` should behave on non-NCCL distributed GPU backends such as federated CUDA communication.
author: RAMitchell

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR reworks multi-GPU quantile sketch exchange to use an NCCL-based tree reduction (via cached NCCL sub-communicators) instead of an allgather-style pattern, aiming to reduce communication volume and avoid per-rank replication. **Changes:** - Add cached NCCL subgroup communicator support (`ncclCommSplit`) in the collective communicator layer. - Implement an NCCL tree reduction + broadcast path for `SketchContainer::AllReduce` that keeps sketch payloads on GPU and prunes after each merge step. - Update MGPU quantile test validation to compare final cut pointers exactly and cut values within a relaxed tolerance. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `src/common/quantile.cu` | Adds NCCL tree reduction/broadcast exchange path for GPU sketch allreduce using cached subgroups and GPU-resident payloads. | | `src/collective/comm.cuh` | Extends `NCCLComm` with cached subgroup support and subgroup metadata (ranks, communicator, stream). | | `src/collective/comm.cu` | Implements `NCCLComm::GetSubgroup` via `ncclCommSplit`, manages subgroup lifetime, and changes root communicator config to blocking. | | `src/collective/nccl_stub.h` | Extends NCCL stub API to include `ncclCommSplit`. | | `src/collective/nccl_stub.cc` | Loads/binds `ncclCommSplit` (dlopen and static-link paths). | | `tests/cpp/common/test_quantile.cu` | Adjusts `MGPUQuantileTest.AllReduceBasic` to validate final cuts (ptrs exact, values tolerant) instead of sketch-level identity. |
